length_bias.note: All six questions clear the 1.20x mean-distractor threshold. No question has correct as longest at the flagging level. Longest_correct_rate=0.0.

per_question.notes: D1 (deliberately programmed): strong misconception targeting intent vs structural encoding. D2 (reflects society accurately): strong, targets the 'AI is neutral mirror' misconception. D3 (AI invented its own bias): plausible but slightly weaker; not absurd.

D1 (historical bias): strong — plausible misapplication of taught concept. D2 (selection bias): plausible mechanism-confusion. D3 (feedback loop): strong — plausible but AI not yet deployed; distinguishable by careful reading.

New question. D1 (labelling bias — inconsistent clinical thresholds): strong — plausible confusion between unequal care access and annotation inconsistency. D2 (selection bias — absent from dataset): strong — directly targets the 'missing data' misconception vs 'present but misleading' reality. D3 (feedback loop — retraining cycle): plausible; a learner who jumps to amplification without noting this is a first-deployment case might select it.

Unchanged from cycle 2. D1 (labelling bias): strong. D2 (feedback loop): plausible timing confusion. D3 (historical bias / photo scarcity): plausible reasoning, addressable by recalling sourcing details in corpus §5.4.

New question. D1 (anchoring+historical, replay framing): strong — correctly flags historical bias as a real concept and automation bias (partially) but wrong pairing; the 'no amplification' clause in D1 directly tests LO5 understanding (loop vs replay). D2 (automation-reviewers+selection-loop): plausible — both terms real but actors and mechanism wrong; weaker on mechanism but not absurd. D3 (confirmation-engineers+anchoring-reviewers): strong — correct bias types but actors deliberately swapped; tests careful matching of bias to actor.

Unchanged from cycle 2. D1 (Seven-Step Trace): strong — named in corpus §6, plausible confusion. D2 (Calibrated Trust Model): plausible adjacent concept. D3 (Encoding Audit Protocol): sounds plausible to a beginner, not a curriculum term.

corpus_los: LO1: Explain what it means for a bias to be 'encoded' into an AI system's training data.
LO2: Describe the four main encoding mechanisms: labelling bias, historical bias, selection bias, and feedback loop amplification.
LO3: Give at least one concrete real-world example for each encoding mechanism.
LO4: Explain how confirmation bias (9.3) and automation bias (9.5) in human practitioners reinforce the encoding problem.
LO5: Identify the difference between a bias that enters once and a feedback loop that makes bias grow over time.
LO6: Name the Judgment Framework as a structured approach covered in Topics 9.7–9.9.
objective_coverage.notes: All 6 LOs covered. q3 (healthcare proxy case) and q4 (facial recognition case) both carry LO3 objective_refs — mechanism name absent from stems in both, requiring example-to-mechanism recall. q5 carries LO4+LO5 jointly: the stem describes a retraining loop and asks why it gets WORSE (LO5), while requiring identification of both cognitive biases (LO4). LO2 coverage is via q2 (labelling mechanism); historical bias (LO2b) and selection bias (LO2c) are exercised implicitly through q3 and q4's correct answers naming the mechanisms, though their objective_refs are tagged LO3 per the rebuild directive.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: All stems plain language. q5 stem mentions 'confirmation bias' and 'automation bias' with Week 9 topic numbers in the question — both are stated prerequisites in corpus §2, so no un-taught knowledge required to parse the stem. q3 stem uses 'proxy measure' which is defined in corpus §7 (used in the healthcare pattern example). q6 tests a fact explicitly stated in corpus §8. No out-of-scope knowledge required for any question.

auto_correction_applied.inline_fix_cycle3: q5 stem extended to test LO5 (feedback loop amplification vs single-pass distinction) alongside LO4; q3 replaced with healthcare proxy case (LO3 example-recall, historical bias answer, mechanism absent from stem); q4 objective_ref retagged to LO3 (facial recognition case unchanged).

verdict: PASS — all 5 checks pass in cycle 3. Positional bias max_share=0.333, length bias longest_correct_rate=0.0, distractor absurd_rate=0.0, all 6 LOs now covered (LO3 by q3+q4 example-recall, LO4+LO5 by q5 combo), beginner scope clean. Carry-over LO3+LO4 gaps from cycle 2 resolved.
